title: Dong Son Bronze Drum
summary: A brilliant symbol of the Red River civilization, demonstrating the superb bronze-casting talent of our ancestors.

slides.text: The Dong Son bronze drum is a special musical instrument and a symbol of the power and wealth of the tribal leaders during the Van Lang era.
slides.imagePrompt: A magnificent bronze Dong Son drum in a tribal setting, golden sunlight highlights its complex patterns, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors, flat design

slides.text: The surface of the drum features vivid images of ancient life: people dancing, beating drums, pounding rice, and war boat fleets.
slides.imagePrompt: Close up of the surface of a Dong Son drum, showing relief patterns of ancient people dancing and rowing boats, children book illustration, cartoon style, simplified patterns

slides.text: At the center of the drum is a multi-pointed star pattern symbolizing the Sun God - the supreme deity in ancient Vietnamese beliefs.
slides.imagePrompt: The star pattern at the center of a bronze drum glowing like the sun, mythical atmosphere, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors, flat design

slides.text: Chim Lac - the legendary bird often seen on the drum's surface, represents the aspiration for freedom and prosperity.
slides.imagePrompt: Stylized Chim Lac birds flying around the sun pattern, ancient Vietnamese aesthetics, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors, flat design

slides.text: Bronze drums were often used in solemn ceremonies. The drum's thunderous sound encouraged the spirit of the soldiers.
slides.imagePrompt: Ancient Vietnamese warriors beating a large bronze drum during a ceremony, bonfire, ritual dance, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.question: Which image lies at the center of the Dong Son bronze drum?
Which bird often appears on the surface of the bronze drum?
What material is the Dong Son bronze drum made of?
